step_by_step_thinking:
Clinical Reasoning:
Given that the five patients developed post-operative wound infections, which suggest a possible mode of transmission via contaminated surfaces or improperly cleaned hands, practicing proper hand washing would be crucial in breaking any chain of infection. Moreover, since we do not know whether the infections were caused by multi-drug resistant organisms requiring targeted treatment approaches, implementing universal precautions including thorough hand washing remains the simplest yet highly effective measure across all scenarios.
Answer: \boxed{D} Based on the available literature, while fumigation could theoretically reduce airborne contaminants and widespread surface disinfection might eliminate many pathogens, they are resource-intensive and less practical compared to consistent adherence to basic infection control methods. Given the established efficacy of hand washing in preventing cross-infections, option D stands out as the most feasible and immediately applicable intervention in a busy post-op ICU environment.
